description: Canonical OSS route-to-authorization contract. Every decorated route in the listed modules must be classified here.

canonical_v1.viewer: allow read and flow execute only when the role domain and object grant match
canonical_v1.developer: allow viewer actions plus create, write, execute, and ingest when the role domain and object grant match
canonical_v1.admin: allow all canonical resource actions when the role domain and object grant match
canonical_v1.owner: allow owned resources except when an API-key or external-identity ceiling is narrower
canonical_v1.direct_share: allow only the shared resource actions encoded by the direct grant
canonical_v1.team_share: allow only the shared resource actions encoded by active team membership
canonical_v1.scoped_role: allow only inside the assigned global, workspace, or project domain
canonical_v1.revoked: deny immediately after the canonical revocation transaction commits

public_or_conditional.viewer: not role-gated; apply the route's public, feature-flag, API-key, or external-auth contract
public_or_conditional.developer: not role-gated; apply the route's public, feature-flag, API-key, or external-auth contract
public_or_conditional.admin: not role-gated; apply the route's public, feature-flag, API-key, or external-auth contract
public_or_conditional.owner: not role-gated; apply the route's public, feature-flag, API-key, or external-auth contract
public_or_conditional.direct_share: no grant beyond the route's public or conditional contract
public_or_conditional.team_share: no grant beyond the route's public or conditional contract
public_or_conditional.scoped_role: no grant beyond the route's public or conditional contract
public_or_conditional.revoked: deny authenticated/private access; public behavior remains public

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: identifier denials are returned as 404; list results omit denied rows
contracts.side_effects: authorize before database mutation, component expansion, or storage I/O
contracts.frontend: flow create, edit, run, deploy, and delete controls use effective flow permissions; public/example reads remain visible

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: private flow identifiers deny as 404; public/configuration routes expose no private identifier
contracts.side_effects: authorize before graph build, execution, file write, posted-code execution, or provider I/O
contracts.frontend: run and advanced-run controls require flow execute; custom-component authoring requires write-class access

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: private flow identifiers deny without confirming existence
contracts.side_effects: authorize before response execution or streaming begins
contracts.frontend: Responses API has no dedicated OSS UI control; API clients must present the same flow execute grant

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: transport errors do not enumerate private flow identifiers
contracts.side_effects: authenticate and authorize before tool execution
contracts.frontend: legacy transport has no standalone control; published MCP controls inherit flow execute visibility

contracts.privacy: project identifier denials are returned as 404
contracts.side_effects: authorize before transport start, configuration mutation, install, or external lookup
contracts.frontend: project MCP publish/configuration controls follow project read/write permissions and are hidden when unsupported

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: private and denied agent identifiers are returned as 404; public agent cards remain discoverable
contracts.side_effects: authorize protected agents before JSON-RPC dispatch; list visibility is filtered before serialization
contracts.frontend: the A2A catalog omits unreadable agents; publish/run controls inherit flow read/execute permissions

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: private flow and job identifiers deny without confirming existence
contracts.side_effects: authorize before build submission, cancellation, streaming, or vertex execution
contracts.frontend: build, cancel, and HITL controls require flow execute and disappear when the effective permission is absent

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.side_effects: authenticate the websocket caller, then authorize flow execute before OpenAI secret lookup, flow description access, voice config creation, provider connection, flow execution, or message persistence
contracts.frontend: voice-session controls require flow:execute and are hidden when voice support or permission is absent; voice:execute is unsupported

contracts.privacy: credential metadata is not returned on denial
contracts.side_effects: authorize before credential lookup or ElevenLabs API I/O
contracts.frontend: voice-picker controls require voice:read and are hidden when the capability is denied or unavailable; voice:execute is unsupported

contracts.privacy: identifier denials are returned as 404; list results omit denied rows
contracts.side_effects: authorize before database mutation or archive storage I/O
contracts.frontend: project create, edit, export, import, and delete controls use effective project permissions

contracts.privacy: collection create/list are personal and owner-scoped to the caller; provider-account identifier denials are returned as 404; lists expose no denied credential metadata
contracts.side_effects: external-access and scoped API-key ceilings run before the canonical owner override; role/plugin enforcement applies when that override is unavailable; authorization precedes credential checks, provider API calls, database mutation, or reconciliation
contracts.frontend: provider-account add, view, edit, and delete controls reflect the canonical personal-owner disposition and remain hidden when a scoped credential is denied

contracts.privacy: deployment identifier denials are returned as 404; lists omit denied rows
contracts.side_effects: authorize before provider API calls, deployment mutation, execution, or reconciliation
contracts.frontend: deployment create, run, edit, rollback, and delete controls use effective deployment permissions

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: private flow identifiers deny as 404; bundled profile pictures are public assets
contracts.side_effects: authorize before storage read, write, or delete
contracts.frontend: legacy flow-file controls inherit effective flow read/write permissions; public profile-picture assets remain visible

contracts.privacy: identifier denials are returned as 404; list results omit denied rows and secret values
contracts.side_effects: authorize before secret persistence, mutation, or environment detection
contracts.frontend: variable create, reveal, edit, and delete controls use effective variable permissions

contracts.privacy: knowledge-base identifier denials are returned as 404; list results omit denied rows
contracts.side_effects: authorize before connector tests, ingestion, cancellation, deletion, or external provider I/O
contracts.frontend: knowledge-base view, create, ingest, cancel, and delete controls use effective knowledge_base permissions

contracts.privacy: file identifier denials are returned as 404; list results omit denied rows
contracts.side_effects: authorize before storage read, write, rename, batch operation, or delete
contracts.frontend: My Files upload, download, rename, batch, and delete controls use effective file permissions

contracts.resource: mcp_server (owner/admin compatibility surface; canonical RBAC unsupported)
contracts.privacy: owner-scoped storage exposes no other user's server names, credentials, or command configuration
contracts.side_effects: owner/admin gate precedes process launch, validation, configuration mutation, or delete
contracts.frontend: show only owner/admin MCP server controls; do not advertise direct/team/scoped RBAC support for this surface

contracts.domain: project folder, then workspace, then global
contracts.privacy: job and flow identifier denials are returned as 404
contracts.side_effects: authorize before stop, HITL resume claim, graph resume, or event attachment
contracts.frontend: workflow status, stop, pending-HITL, resume, and reattach controls require effective flow read/execute

contracts.privacy: unknown or non-public flows are returned as 404
contracts.side_effects: verify public eligibility and request limits before execution
contracts.frontend: public workflow invocation is exposed only for flows explicitly published as public
